primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables for foreign currency risk hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Directly governs hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items in consolidated financial statements, with specific exception for intragroup monetary items with foreign exchange exposure not eliminated on consolidation
Governs classification of financial instruments as financial liabilities or equity, which determines the nature of dividend obligations and receivables

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations and constraints on designating hedged risk for intragroup items
Determines whether foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items are eliminated on consolidation, which is the condition for the paragraph 6.3.6 exception

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses members' shares and redemption features in cooperative entities, not directly applicable to hedge accounting of dividend receivables
Addresses dividend payable recognition and measurement, not hedge accounting eligibility for foreign currency risk

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements
General hedge accounting prohibition for intragroup transactions in consolidated statements

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Paragraph 6.3.6 of IFRS 9 provides an explicit exception allowing the foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items (such as dividend receivables) to qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements when the foreign exchange exposure is not fully eliminated on consolidation under IAS 21

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intragroup items in consolidated statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Paragraph 6.3.5 of IFRS 9 generally prohibits hedge accounting for intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements, as transactions between entities in the same group are not with external parties
